title: General Legal Aid * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > General Legal Aid * Native American Community 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers Migrants Native American Community Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON NATIVE AMERICAN PROGRAM NATIVE AMERICAN PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides civil legal services to Indian tribes, Native organizations, and individual Native people for issues involving Indian law or tribal law or otherwise related to status as a tribal member. Does not handle domestic relations or housing matters (these types of cases are referred to local Legal Aid offices). Does not accept cases against Indian tribes. Current case priority categories include access to tribal courts, tribal court enhancement, protection of tribal sovereignty and self-government, Indian Child Welfare Act issues, tribal enrollment, issues involving trust land and resources, educational issues, health care issues, probate and estate planning, public benefits (where impacted by an applicant/recipient's Indian status), and domestic violence. Eligibility: Indian tribes and groups, and Native individuals. Must meet low-income eligibility requirements. Hours: By appointment only (503) 223-9483 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
